summary: Dedicated header, action, sizing, and body chrome for data-visualization cards whose layouts evolve independently from settings sections.
useWhen: A card contains a chart, data-visualization legend, metric visualization, or a specialized data-viz composition.
A data-visualization card needs a heading and optional header actions while retaining a flexible body for its composing component.
Several chart-specific cards need one shell boundary that can evolve without changing settings sections.
avoidWhen: The card contains settings or editable form content; use the settings card composition.
The surface is ordinary content without data visualization; use a product-owned layout rather than data-viz card chrome.
The shell would own chart-specific layout, hover synchronization, legends, or empty-state policy; keep those in the composing data-viz card.

alternatives.when: The surface contains editable settings rather than data visualization.
alternatives.reason: CardSetting owns the persistent controlled settings-section workflow.

alternatives.when: The composition is the standard donut chart plus synchronized legend.
alternatives.reason: CardDataVizDonut composes this shell with donut-specific regions and hover synchronization.

alternatives.when: The composition is the standard line chart plus static horizontal legend.
alternatives.reason: CardDataVizLine composes this shell with line-specific regions and disables decorative legend hover.

accessibility: Provide a concise visible heading that identifies the visualization; the shell renders it as a section heading.
Header actions must provide their own accessible names and communicate any controlled state or popup relationship.
The shell does not add landmark or chart semantics; composing charts retain their own accessible names and descriptions.
Do not duplicate chart data in the heading. Provide an accessible summary through the chart or adjacent composition when visual encoding alone is insufficient.
states: The shell owns default data-visualization surface chrome only; loading, empty, error, selected, and interactive states belong to the composing card or chart.
The actions slot collapses when empty so headings retain the available header width.
responsiveBehavior: The selected sm, md, or lg width sets the matching token-based minimum height while max-width prevents overflow from a narrower parent.
The parent layout chooses card width, wrapping, stacking, and breakpoint changes; the shell does not switch sizes automatically.
The body is a flexible column so specialized data-viz cards can allocate remaining height between charts, legends, and supporting content.

customElements: Place header controls in the actions slot and data-visualization composition in the default slot.
react: Pass header controls with slot="actions" and keep chart state in the composing React component.
angular: Project header controls with slot="actions" and keep chart state in the composing Angular component.
